desc:
The trail continues to the east and west here, past heavy woodlands all around.
To the north, nothing special attracts your attention besides the abnormally
thick growth of vines and tree limbs. To your south however, there is a grassy
field. A pile of stones that looks out of place makes you take notice. Should
you investigate or write it off to coincidence?
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail bends here, leading south or west. To the north lies a great forest
containing massive trees overgrown with vines and bushes. A large fire in this
woodland would surely be visible for many miles, and would burn for many a
night. The gloomy blackness of the forest stares back at you, seeming to invite
you in. You cannot be sure, but you think you see many glowing eyes peering out
at you from behind the tree trunks.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail you are on is hard packed dirt. It leads through a dense forest,
choked with vines and bushes amid the massive tree trunks. The trail leads north
and east here, past a dark woodland. To the west is a small clearing, looking
uninhabited at the moment, but signs of encampment mark the area in the form of
an old fire pit. Should you investigate or leave it undisturbed?
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path continues from the east to west here. Another trail leads into the
south. Whoever makes their residence in these parts is a sturdy fellow for sure.
The forest that spreads its breadth around you is dark and gloomy. Thick
branches and vines grow everywhere, some even encroaching on the trail itself.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail rises and falls slowly with the rolling plains. Bushes and tall
grasses brush across the horizon. The dirt along the trail seems to have been
disturbed by the passing of others. The land slowly rises to a mountain range in
the northwest. The trail continues its motion to the north and south.
name: Rolling Trail

desc:
The trail runs north, crossing a series of low hills heading towards a large
range of mountains far to the north. The track slowly descends as it wanders
through the plains towards the south. The path is well kept with small rocks
marking its passage. The rest of the hill continues to the west, but slowly
merges with a range of greyish-white hills far to the northwest.
name: Rolling Trail

desc:
Water trickles from many tiny gullies, meeting to form a small stream. The flow
of water is not yet continuous. But the ground in the shallow ditch seems to
stay wet. The trail continues to the north and west, crossing the dense carpet
of grass. The plains continue far to the east and south.
name: Trail along the Stream

desc:
Travelling this nicely kept mountain trail, zigzagging between and around
boulders, fissures and rocky outcroppings. A majestic granite ridge rises into
the sky a bit north of here. Streams and small hills mix in this relatively flat
terrain, several smaller streams slowly descend to the east. A neatly kept trail
continues to the west.
name: Zigzag Trail

desc:
The trail is well kept and its borders are lined with small stones retrieved
from the rocky ridge that lies to the north-west. The trail continues to the
east and south. The land rises and falls to the north and south. The land rises
to the northwest. Trees dot the plains and increase in frequency to the
northwest.
name: Trail in the Wilderness

desc:
The trail continues to the north and south. The terrain gradually rises to the
west as the trees increase in number. Through the scraggly oak trees and tall
underbrush, a tall hill can be seen reaching for the sky. A larch-wood glade
with heavy underbrush covers the ground to the west. Various trails created by
animals cross between the bushes.
name: Trail in the Wilderness

desc:
The trail follows the flow of the land and bends to the north and west. A few
scattered oaks dot the plains, marking the beginning of a vast forest to the
southwest. Gently rolling plains cover the horizon to the north and east. Tall
grasses flow along with any breeze. Clumps of brush and an occasional tree break
up the monotony.
name: Trail in the Wilderness

desc:
Bushes, shrubs and high grass are threatening to overgrow this trail. Some big
tree roots have already cracked it up. A lone oak is growing besides the trail
is here and a manmade birds nest is nailed to the trunk about twelve feet from
the ground. The hills stretch west as far as you can see.
name: Rambling Trail

desc:
The trail turns to the west and to the north here. To the north a tavern stands
next to the trail, welcoming travellers. Trees grow more numerous around the
trail here as well as bushes and other larger plants. Animal tracks are mixed
with the tracks of humanoids and cart tracks on the ground.
name: Rambling Trail

desc:
An alehouse stands to the east. On the sign, a worn painting of an old axe can
be seen. The building seems small compared to those of the great elven cities to
the south. However, it is a welcome sight on the long trail. Shrubs and bushes
decorate the western edge. The worn path continues to the north and south.
name: In Front of a Wooden Building

desc:
Large stones have been placed in various spots crossing the shallow stream. A
few lie covered with moss. Thick brush and a few large oaks keep the crossing
private. The trail leads north into a series of foothills, while to the south it
crosses a vast plain. The foothills wind their way from the north, to the west,
before continuing south again.
name: Stream Crossing

desc:
The trail appears to have been cut through the limestone between two valleys.
Steep vertical cliff sides have been worn by water, which drips slowly even now.
Rocks litter the sides of the trail. The valley to the east, and the one to the
south, seems to open up to a land much smoother than the rocky hills here. An
occasional crevice leads even deeper into the hills, many too small to enter.
name: Limestone Trail

desc:
Small shrubs and young trees line the trail, like heralds, waiting for the
arrival of their king. Grass flows gently along the plains far to the east and
south. A series of deep hills line the base of a high plateau to the north and
west. The trail cuts follows a limestone valley to the west, hopefully, more
easily travelled than the rest of the hills seem to be.
name: Bend in the Trail

desc:
Various bushes grow beside the trail, and a line of young trees dot the plains
nearby. A deep series of washed-out limestone valleys lead deep into a series of
hills to the west, which eventually lead to a high plain to the west. The trail
continues along the foothills to the north, while the trail leads around the
hills to the south.
name: Trail along the Base of Low Hills

desc:
Grass grows thickly along the sides of the trail. Large bushes present hiding
places to predators of all kinds. Flowers have taken root in the rich soil that
has been washed down from the high plains to the west. Foothills begin the
climb. The trail continues to follow the foot of the ridge to the north and
south.
name: Trail along the Base of Low Hills

desc:
A narrow, worn trail snakes its way through the hilly countryside. All about,
towering hills rise up in various shapes and sizes. The trail continues north
and south, running parallel to the ridge west of here. Some tiny flowers grow
beside the trail amidst the lush green grass.
name: A Trail beside the Ridge

desc:
The trail climbs a slope here, and to the west the land slopes upward into the
foothills of this small mountain range, which whilst spectacular, is no match to
the lofty peaks of Ered Luin, far west of here. To the south the trail winds
between massive boulders and piles of rubble, travel both east and west is
possible with little difficulty. To the north, the trail runs down a fairly
steep slope.
name: A Faltering Trail in the Rising Mountains

desc:
Off to the west of here the trail narrows, and almost disappears, as it marches
into the rising foothills of the mountains. The ground underfoot is quite rocky,
but still numerous bushes and small shrubs line either side of the path, and the
everpresent sound of birds and insects can be heard.
name: On a Faltering Trail

desc:
The trail here is only wide enough for one or two to ride or walk abreast.
Underfoot, the ground is rocky, and the surface fairly uneven, and the path
slopes upward as it marches to the southwest from here. To the north the footing
appears to improve, as the path climbs steadily downward.
name: Tiny Trail

desc:
The trail grows narrower here than it is to the north, though the same small
bushes and shrubs still line either side of it, and it has the appearance of a
pathway wrought by mortal hand. It slopes steadily upward to the south, and
becomes rockier in that direction, whereas to the north it widens out and grows
steadily wider and smoother.
name: Tiny Trail

desc:
Small bushes and trees line the trail and you can hear the sound of many birds
singing within their boughs. The width of this trail could easily accomodate
four or five horsemen abreast, but to the south it becomes narrower, steeper and
more rocky. However, it allows easy access to the forest to the north.
name: At a Branch in the Trail

desc:
The ground underfoot is quite poor and rocky, so that only a few small, sickly
shrubs and some thin grasses cling to life here. And much of the vegetation has
been worn away by the feet of passing travellers, using this route for their
journey from the Blue Mountains to the lowlands to the east.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The rocky soil here is a pain to walk on for either mount or walker. A little
thicket of bushes grows here next to a very large mountain standing to the
south. Much life fills this thicket including snakes, rabbits, squirrels, and
other small rodents. A journey to the south looks very tiresome, as the trail
begins to climb up a steep slope.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Far to the west you see patches of green grass and hills of lush bushes. Here
however, there seems to be only a cracked rocky soil that makes walking
difficult for either mount or walker. Thanks to the white stones lying beside
the trail here you can follow it. It must have been the dwarves that marked the
trail this way in order to follow it even on such a rocky terrain. Northwards
lie some more flat-lands, and to the south a small range of hills lead into the
eastern Ered Luin. Somewhere in the north you hear the sound of flowing water,
probably the Nan Duin.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You find yourself on the top of a small hill that gives you a view of the
surroundings. You can see a huge forest to the west, tall and dark it seems to
be, and also to your east you can spot a great forest. The trail is broad and
wellmarked and is easy to follow. To the south west you can see a small farm in
the distance. It is in fair shape, and is obviously inhabited. Flat land reveals
itself on your east side, and you can barely see the swift Nan Duin flowing in
the north.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You are in a wide expanse of plains now, with the path clearly followed in the
middle. You can see two big forest to the east and west here. The one to the
east seems rather large, but you are so far away from it that you really can't
tell that much about it at all. The one to your west side however, looks dark
and gloomy, with many large trees and snaking vines. What evils lurk in that
section of woodland? You hear a river flowing to the north, and also spy a small
bridge in that direction.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path goes ever on, and now you are right outside a small farm. These fields
have been somewhat cultivated. Not carefully tended and tilled as the farms in
the Shire are, but worked on nonetheless. Rocks have been removed as has the
annual grass. In its place grow a few green shoots indicating vegetables. A gate
is to your south, probably the entry into a small farm, and you will reach a
cosy home after crossing what appears to be a pasture.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The path makes a smooth bend here and turns east and south. However, the west
holds a very dark forest. Also to the north is a forest, seeming to reach around
and encompass you. This seems to be a transition area between the open hills and
plains, where it merges with the dark gloominess of the forest. Small animals
grace the plains, and similar creatures occupy the forest. These are not what
concerns you however; the ones that hide within the bounds of the forest are of
more concern. Should you enter?
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
Traversing the land between rolling hills, you can still follow the trail
easily. It contains nothing of unusual properties, just wavy grass and small
animals. The way runs north-south here, opening into more plains-type land in
front and in back of you. Scrambling up the gentle forest would be easy, if not
for a crumbling ridgeline that parallels the valley. It lies on your west side,
and would be impossible to climb up without dislodging many rocks.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Small bushes and trees line the trail and you can hear the sound of many birds
singing within their boughs. The width of this trail could easily accomodate
four or five horsemen abreast, and was obviously created to allow easy access
into the great forest. Off to the east lies a large clearing and looks like a
perfect place to stop and rest.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Small bushes and trees line the trail and you can hear the sound of many birds
singing within their boughs. The width of this trail could easily accommodate
four or five horsemen abreast, and was obviously created to allow easy access
into the great forest. To the east the trees become more frequent and the trail
narrows.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail narrows and is almost completely engulfed by the forest. Great trees
tower along either side of you as you carefully make your way through the
forest. Wild animals can be heard from every direction which cautions you to
navigate carefully. The trail leads west and east.
name: Trail through the Great Forest

desc:
The trail narrows and is almost completely engulfed by the forest. Great trees
tower along either side of you as you carefully make your way through the
forest. Wild animals can be heard from every direction which cautions you to
navigate carefully. The trail leads west and south.
name: Trail through the Great Forest

desc:
The trail narrows and is almost completely engulfed by the forest. Great trees
tower along either side of you as you carefully make your way through the
forest. Wild animals can be heard from every direction which cautions you to
navigate carefully. The trail leads south and a sturdy bridge can be seen to the
north.
name: Trail through the Great Forest

desc:
The trail narrows and is almost completely engulfed by the forest. Great trees
tower along either side of you as you carefully make your way through the
forest. Wild animals can be heard from every direction which cautions you to
navigate carefully. The trail leads west and north.
name: Trail through the Great Forest

desc:
The trail narrows and is almost completely engulfed by the forest. Great trees
tower along either side of you as you carefully make your way through the
forest. Wild animals can be heard from every direction which cautions you to
navigate carefully. The trail leads north and east.
name: Trail through the Great Forest

desc:
Although very old, you can feel the strength of this bridge as you walk upon it.
Its sturdy wooden beams look to be in excellent condition considering their age,
and make no sound whatsoever as they shift slightly to accomodate your weight.
The bridge spans across the river and leads south into the great forest, and to
the north there are open plains.
name: Old Sturdy Bridge

desc:
Unlike the terrain on the south side of the river, vast hilly plains cover the
entire horizon. The small rolling hills are covered with long yellow-brown
grasses, some even waist high. Bare spots of earth and stone top off the
occasional hill accenting their variety of shapes and sizes. The trail continues
to the west and to the south you can see a bridge.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Hills continue to stretch out as far as you can see. The grass on the hilly
slopes hypnotically sways as strong breezes swirl around from multiple
directions. To the south thick brambles makes it impossible to travel in that
direction, but a steady murmur and the occasional splash confirm that the river
is nearby. The trail continues to the east and west.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Hills continue to stretch out as far as you can see, yet the path is easy to
follow here. The grass on the hilly slopes hypnotically sways as strong breezes
swirl around from multiple directions. To the south thick brambles make it
impossible to travel in that direction, but a steady murmur and the occasional
splash confirm that the river is nearby.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Hills continue to stretch out in all directions. The grass on the hilly slopes
hypnotically sways as strong breezes swirl around from multiple directions. The
hills continue on to the south towards the river, and to the north towards
higher ground. The path continues to the north and south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Hills still stretch out in all directions, but their numbers decline and each
hill seems to be the size of a small mountain. The land has much more definition
here and it is no longer easy to keep watch in all directions. A high ridge to
the distant north traces the horizon and several odd formations along its edge,
not quite natural, catches your eye. The path bends to the east and south here.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
Hills still stretch out in all directions, but their numbers decline and each
hill seems to be the size of a small mountain. The land has much more definition
here and it is no longer easy to keep watch in all directions. A high ridge to
the distant north traces the horizon and several odd formations along its edge,
not quite natural, catch your eye. From this viewpoint they look to be outposts
of some sort, but you cannot tell for sure.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
An assortment of different sized rocks blanket the entire floor of the valley
making footing difficult and you take each step with care. A cold damp chill
swells around you as you settle into a comfortable stride in the centre of the
valley bed. Plains open up to the north and the valley continues on to the east.
name: Rocky Valley

desc:
An assortment of different sized rocks blanket the entire floor of the valley,
making footing difficult, and you take each step with care. A cold damp chill
swells around you as you settle into a comfortable stride in the centre of the
valley bed. The trail continues to the north and east.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You make your way past the loose scree and begin to climb the steep winding path
that leads up the side of the mountain. In a few areas the rock face steepens
and juts out, forcing you to make short vertical climbs to regain the path and
continue onward. To the west lies a shallow valley while the worn trail
continues upwards.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Although the trail is not nearly as steep as it is below, the grade is
sufficient to keep you cautious as you search for solid footing. High grass
grows all about and seems to be the only sign of any vegetation in the vicinity.
The grass's brown edges and yellow stalks indicate the problem with the ground's
impermability to water, and if it were not for the occasional overflow of the
nearby river nothing could grow here at all.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
These floodplains consist entirely of soft topsoil, silt, and other debris that
the river deposits here when it overflows its banks. This combination of soil
and river-life provides perfect fertile land allowing bushes, shrubs, and
flowers to thrive and flourish along the plains' northern and western outskirts.
The western side of the plains show deep linear gouges in the land, as if large
heavy objects had been consistantly dragged across from the river. You try to
come up with some way these could be naturally occuring, since the only obvious
exit to the south is by no means a possible route to take heavy equipment, but
you are unable to fathom any explanation. To the east you can see many rocks; it
seems like you could cross the river without a boat here.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
You carefully step on the boulders while the water trickles past you. Through
the crystal clear water you can see the many different species of fish and other
river life living within the shallows. Tiny ripples from the dew dripping off
the surrounding plantlife are the only blemishes on the glassy surface of these
tranquil waters.
name: Stepping Stones across the River

desc:
Small rocks cover the ground all around you and make small crackling sounds as
you move upon them. Small patches of ground quartz line the areas closer to the
water, bubbling with moisture. To the east you see flat ground, covered with
larger rocks and a few huge boulders which dominate your view. The trail ends
here, but it looks like it continues on the other side of the river. To the west
you can see a river, still you might be able to enter without a boat.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Massive boulders seem to be everywhere, making it difficult to move quickly
around this terrain. An uneasiness comes over you as you feel that this place is
much too quiet, seemingly void of life. You would not give it another thought if
it were not for the strange scrapings you see on the lower portion of the rocks,
and the faint scent of musk that fills the air. The trail is a pain to follow
for you and you have to take it easy around here.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Massive boulders seem to be everywhere, making it difficult to move quickly
around this terrain. An uneasiness comes over you as you feel that this place is
much too quiet, seemingly void of life. You would not give it another thought if
it were not for the strange scrapings you see on the lower portion of the rocks,
and the faint scent of musk that fills the air. The trail branches here, with
travel possible to the east, west and south.
name: Along a Branching Trail

desc:
Giant boulders tower over you blocking your passage to the south. Their masses
cast shadows all about the ground and it is difficult to see if any evil lurks
within them. Off to the west you see the edge of a sparse forest and you can
hear the sound of many different animals rustling amongst the trees. It looks
like the trail leads into an opening to the north here.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
With predominantly younger trees spaced many meters apart, this section of the
forest is quite bright and welcoming making you feel at ease as you stroll
deeper into the forest. To the east the trees thin and then disappear
completely, giving way to the a trail. To the south the trees grow much closer
together, effectively sheltering the ground from the elements as well as
blocking out much of the light, yet there seems to be a path you can follow
here.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
The forest floor beneath these green giants is thick with shadows, the faint
streamers of light let through by gaps between the thick burly branches rapidly
disappear at the first signs of twilight. Eyes dart out from every dark corner,
mischievous and curious, watching your every move with the utmost interest. The
trail continues to the south and north.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
The forest floor beneath these green giants is thick with shadows, the faint
streamers of light let through by gaps between the thick burly branches rapidly
disappear at the first signs of twilight. Eyes dart out from every dark corner,
mischievous and curious, watching your every move with the utmost interest. The
trail continues to the south and north.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
The forest floor beneath these green giants is thick with shadows, the faint
streamers of light let through by gaps between the thick burly branches rapidly
disappear at the first signs of twilight. Eyes dart out from every dark corner,
mischievous and curious, watching your every move with the utmost interest. The
trail bends to the east and north here.
name: Bend in the Forest Trail

desc:
You stumble across what seems to be a small path meandering through the rows of
trees. It seems to be very old but well used. It must be the main trail from the
southern lands into the Blue mountains to the northwest. You seem to be able to
distiguish the path as it leads south and west, continuing through the towering
trees.
name: Bend in the Forest Trail

desc:
You try your best to keep sight of the signs of the old forest path. Giant trees
surround you, towering and intimidating, forcing you to keep aware of their
presence as well as the creatures that they might shelter. You seem to
temporarily lose your way, but ahead to the south and north you can once again
see small traces of the ancient byway.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
The thick thorny bushes fade away, replaced by soft dark earth and small green
and yellow bushes growing little red berries. Off to the west the trees once
again are thick, and the murmer of rushing water can be heard. The byway
continues over a small leaf-covered hill to the north and south.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
You are walking on what seems to be a well-travelled path in the forest. The
trees here are huge, blocking the sun, even when the sun is at its highest. The
trail continues to the east and west. To the far north you can catch the sight
of hills, while southwards there's more forest.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
The Nan Duin continues to lay out before you as it pushes its way through the
Blue Mountains. To the north is the edge of a large hut, while to the east and
west the river continues. The hut is inacessible from here, as a large hedge is
preventing you from getting out of the river at that point. The water gives the
air a clean smell as it bubbles along.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The Nan Duin stretches far to the east and west through the Blue Mountains. To
the north and south directions, the riverbank quickly is engulfed by dense
forest. The gloom of the forest is a sudden change from the openness of the
river itself. Should you leave the relative safety of the water in order to gain
a foot-hold on land?
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
You continue along the blue water of the of Nan Duin as it stretches far to the
east and west beyond the horizon. The river bank to the north reveals a
beautiful forest with trees shooting higher than the tallest watch-tower.
Animals can be seen and heard moving about among the trees. Some are quite
harmless, as the deer, but others may lurk in the dense growth that have more
sinister mindsets.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
Many stones rise out of the water because the river seems to be a bit more
shallow here. Probably because of a slight land-shift, the uneveness of the
riverbed will soon undergo its natural geologic fate. As with all obstructions
in a waterway such as this, the deformity is either smoothed over by the
current, or the water just goes around it. Northwards you spy a large forest,
spanning much of the land area in this region. There seems to be a break in the
dense woods close to the shore.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
Still running east-west, the Nan Duin flows along swiftly here. The water is
cool, signifying its origins in higher altitudes. Small fish can be seen
swimming around the rocks on the bottom. To the north, a break in the forest has
created a nice open area on the riverbank. Though small, it is a brief respite
before plunging into the gloominess that the main forest holds. Eastwards, the
river flows under a bridge, and to the south are rolling plains of grass.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The Nan Duin stretches far to the east and west through the Blue Mountains. To
both the south and north you see trees growing. They do not form dense forests
like the ones further west, rather small groves that offer shade on a sunny day,
or shelter from rain and snow. The river flows along swiftly here, making white
peaks as the water runs over the uneven floor of the streambed.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river here seems unusually shallow. Perhaps it is the abundance of rocks in
the water. Whatever the reason, you would be advised to take care that your boat
does not get stuck on the high points of the ground. Trees border the river to
both the north and the south, though the ones in the north seem much more dense.
Southwards there are plains just past the trees.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The Nan Duin takes a slight bend to your south, only to skirt a large
outcropping of trees. It then resumes its rigid east-west course through the
Ered Luin. In the north, you see a large forest, it is dark and gloomy except
for the small landing beach right in front of you. Cries of animals come from
the dark woods, and you suspect not all of them are happy.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river continues to meander lazily through the rough terrain of the great
forests and mountains that cover this land. Its slow steady pace and sheer
determination has kept it flowing along this route for thousands of years,
bringing life to forests and creatures alike that need its fresh water to
survive.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river continues to meander lazily through the rough terrain of the great
forests and mountains that cover this land. Its slow steady pace and sheer
determination has kept it flowing along this route for thousands of years,
bringing life to forests and creatures alike that need its fresh water to
survive.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river continues to meander lazily through the rough terrain of the great
forests and mountains that cover this land. Its slow steady pace and sheer
determination have kept it flowing along this route for thousands of years,
bringing life to forests and creatures alike that need its fresh water to
survive.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river continues to meander lazily through the rough terrain of the great
forests and mountains that cover this land. Its slow steady pace and sheer
determination has kept it flowing along this route for thousands of years,
bringing life to forests and creatures alike that need its fresh water to
survive. Ahead to the east the river narrows slightly, and you see a sturdy
wooden bridge spanning from bank to bank.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river continues to meander lazily through the rough terrain of the great
forests and mountains that cover this land. Its slow steady pace and sheer
determination has kept it flowing along this route for thousands of years,
bringing life to forests and creatures alike that need its fresh water to
survive. To the west the river narrows slightly, and you see a sturdy wooden
bridge spanning from bank to bank. The river flows from west to east.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
Here the rivers collide, creating rough waters as the two massive currents meet.
The waves build, and you can no longer see the bottom of the river as the silt
and sand whirling around obscures your view. The rivers flow in from the north
and west, and leaves south.
name: River Junction

desc:
The river continues south here, beginning to get a little choppy. The calm
glass-like surface no longer exists, but the waters are still calm enough that
you feel at peace. To the south the river seems aggitated. The river flows from
north to south.
name: River

desc:
The river continues south here, slow and effortless. Through the crystal clear
water you can see the many different species of fish and other river life. Tiny
ripples from the dew dripping off the surrounding plantlife are the only
blemishes on the glassy surface of these tranquil waters. The river flows from
north to south.
name: River

desc:
The river continues south here, slow and effortless. Through the crystal clear
water you can see the many different species of fish and other river life living
within the shallows. Tiny ripples from the dew dripping off the surrounding
plantlife are the only blemishes on the glassy surface of these tranquil waters.
The river flows from north to south.
name: Calm River

desc:
The river begins to build up velocity as it flows down from the higher ground to
the north. The water is very dark and you are careful to avoid the numerous
boulders that jut out of the water's surface. The river flows in from the north
and leaves east.
name: Whitewaters

desc:
The swift waters coming in from the west are steered through these narrow turns
in the river by huge bedrock banks. The waters appear almost alive as they
violently splash up against the barriers that force them to alter their path.
The river flows in from the west and leaves south. A large -sign- is tacked to
the rock wall.
name: Whitewaters

desc:
The river straightens out and widens here, slowing the currents and settling the
waters. The river once again begins to flow at its usual slow and steady pace,
and the debris held aloft within its waters slowly sinks, and is deposited along
the rivers edge. The river flows from north to south.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river meanders lazily through the rough terrain of the great forests that
surround it. You see huge cliff walls on either side of you leading up to the
mass of trees that hover over you like immense giants. The river flows in from
the north and leaves east.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river meanders lazily through the rough terrain of the great forests that
surround it. You see huge walls jut up on either side of you leading straight up
to sharp edges that line the cliff's length. You see some odd looking markings
on the upper edges of the cliff walls and wonder who or what could have made
them. The river flows in from the west and leaves south.
name: River below the Cliffs

desc:
The river meanders lazily through the rough terrain of the great forests that
surround it. You see huge cliff walls on either side of you leading up to the
mass of trees that hover over you like immense giants. The river flows in from
the north and leaves east.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river meanders lazily through the rough terrain of the great forests that
surround it. You see huge cliff walls on either side of you leading up to the
mass of trees that hover over you like immense giants. The river flows in from
the west and leaves south.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river meanders lazily through the rough terrain of the great forests that
surround it. You see huge cliff walls on either side of you leading up to the
mass of trees that hover over you like immense giants.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river is now wide and fairly slow, so it is probably best to let your craft
float if you are headed down the river, and just sit back and relax. To the
north you can see a pretty dense forest, and you can see a huge old tree at the
northern bank. Some timber lies around the shore line. The river makes a turn
here and continues to the south and west.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
You are on a small tributary which flows slowly to the south. The water is very
clear and you can easily make out different sized stones on the bottom.
Interspersed among the rocks, are green aquatic plants swaying in the current.
name: A Small Tributary of the River

desc:
The forest consists of oaks and many varieties of fir trees. Broad trunks reach
far into the sky, their interlaced branches creating a thick canopy. Light seems
to penetrate due to the fact that there still is plenty of undergrowth surviving
along the ground. Travel is much easier along the base of the pine trees, where
the fallen needles have made it difficult for other plants to grow. The forest
seems to grow even deeper to the west. However, a wide plain can be seen through
the trees to the north and east.
name: Forest Edge

desc:
Ancient oak trees spread their branches in an almost grabbing fashion. They mark
this forest as very old. Some underbrush is evident, but most of it has been
eaten by animals. A big owl's nest is prominent in one of the trees. The forest
grows thicker and darker the more west you go.
name: Old Gnarled Oaks

desc:
The broken limestone ridge separates the lowland plains to the east from the
high plains to the northwest. Varied bushes provide cover for the various
animals that inhabit the local area. Bare patches of solid limestone sit
intermixed with areas covered with plant life. A trail circles around this rocky
patch of ground from the south to the east.
name: On the Border of Foothills

desc:
The land gently drifts up and down, slowly, gently. Grasses completely cover the
ground, patches of large bushes and a few isolated trees manage to keep the view
from becoming monotonous. Once in a while, a small mound of dirt stands alone,
announcing the home of some burrowing animal. The plains seem endless and
continue on far to the north and west.
name: Gentle Plains

desc:
An egg shaped bump of limestone has forced its way to the surface. A small strip
of stone stretches across the plains, almost 100 yards long, creating an small
area where the grass refuses to grow. A large patch of bushy vegetation has
found a home, where the limestone has channelled the rain water. A single tree
has found a place to plant its roots along this bald spot, providing shade
during the afternoon. A few other trees can be spotted along the grass covered
plains.
name: Gentle Plains

desc:
Thick, long, wavy grasses lead up to this sinkhole. Grasses continue growing
along the sides. Thick brush grows amongst a tumble of boulders gathered at the
bottom. The grasslands continue on in all directions. The plains are broken up
by scattered trees and large clumps of brush.
name: Small Sinkhole

desc:
A twisted pine stands writhing in pain. The trunk bends with unusual angles, the
branches stretch from awkward angles. A few well-developed boughs seem to
provide the tree with enough enrichment to survive. The grassy fields provide a
comforting contrast to the ancient trees. The plains give way to a rocky
limestone outcroppings to the south and east.
name: Twisted Pine

desc:
Fir-trees lie scattered throughout the limestone outcroppings. They have found
cracks in the ground to plant their roots in. The roots of one large tree has
split one of the larger boulders. Ferns, small berry bushes and many climbing
vines cover the ground following the furrows of soil between the limestone
rocks. This is a range of hills going from the south to the north, splitting the
flat plains east and west of here in two parts.
name: Patch of Fir-Wood

desc:
This valley splits the range of hills that goes from the south into the north.
The stone bed floor of the valley completely covers up the once free flowing
stream. Water still flows beneath the rocks and stones, having carved out a tiny
new canyon of its own. The tracks of many small animals can be seen crossing
through the light silt that has gathered on the stones all around. The land
rises to the north and south, but hints of a vast plain can be seen through the
jagged limestone peaks to the east and west.
name: Stone-bedded Valley in the Hills

desc:
A stream flows down from the foothills, the water splitting apart, with some
flowing into the grasslands to the south. Most of the flow continues to the
east. Large quantities of shrubs and bushes grow tightly together on the
southern bank, nourished by the fresh stream water. A line of hills gathers
around the quiet plains to the north and west.
name: Clear Stream from the Foothills

desc:
All sorts of bushes, ferns and other low growing plants completely cover the
ground. The ground is saturated with water flowing out of the hills to the
northeast. Puddles of shallow water lie in shallow places upon the earth. A few
large prints can be seen in the soft dirt. Some large animal has been chewing on
the local foliage.
name: Green Shrubbery

desc:
Deep gullies dig into the plains above, revealing many layers of limestone.
Thick grass grows along the grooves cut into the stone, where eroded topsoil has
settled. Limestone rocks litter the bottom making travel difficult. The hillside
continues to the west, the way obscured by the rough terrain. A faint trail
follows along the north-south ridge to the east.
name: Eroded Hillside

desc:
The grasses flow gently with the passing winds, while the bushes bend towards
the ground when the wind picks up. A few trees stand strong, but a groan can be
heard on an occasion. The fields flow gently downhill to the southeast, where
they quickly give way to an eroding hillside. A great view of the coastal plains
of the Gulf of Lhun can be seen from the edge of the hills.
name: Windy Escarpment

desc:
Ancient furrows lie covered in weeds, while briars grow freely on the formerly
bare earth. A pile of sod lies near a circle of stones protecting a circle of
ashes. Wild grasses have begun to cover the clearing, similar to the way they
have almost completely covered an ancient trail leading towards a series of
hills in the south. The flatness of the plains continues on in all other
directions, infrequently pierced by a lonely tree.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
Vegetation has found few places to flourish within these limestone hills. A few
bushes have found pooled topsoil to spread their roots. The thick grasses from
the plains above grows in clumps. The slowly eroding plains abruptly end here,
descending to coastal plains of the Gulf of Lhun. The limestone ridge continues
to the south and east.
name: Limestone Hills

desc:
The smoothly flowing grasses of the highland plains end along the northern and
eastern edges of this field. Erosion has cut many grooves through the rich dirt,
revealing the crumbling limestone that decorates the hills along the edge of the
plains. A few trees have managed to climb into the sky despite the constant
battle with the winds. Bushes grow in large clumps all along the plains to the
west and south.
name: Windy Escarpment

desc:
Deep crevices slice through the limestone, created by torrents of water rushing
down from the mountains far to the northwest. Small boulders and rocks lie
scattered about, left there when the swiftly flowing waters finally subsided.
Roots of many large bushes reach deep into the ground, the upper areas brazenly
exposed to the elements.
name: Low Foothills

desc:
The hills rise in height the further west you travel. Far off to the west you
can see that the hills have turned into a small mountain peak. A trail made for
easy travel has been made to the east of here. The lands are very fertile here
making it a good place for wildlife.
name: Entering the Foothills

desc:
Grass grows all over these hills, as do trees. The trees do not grow together in
a forest but instead are randomly spread around in small groves. This gives the
region a strange unfinished look. To the east the hills become a large stone
plateau. More hills cover the land to the south.
name: Uplands

desc:
Thick clumps of grass protected by briars lie scattered throughout the grazed
plains. A few round brown platters can be seen sitting on the ground. Wild
grasses grow thicker to the south and east. A few lonely trees stand against the
cold wind gusts that comes swirling down from the heights of the Blue Mountains
to the northwest. A trail can be seen west of here.
name: Grazed Lands

desc:
Blades of the long grass lie pointing to the south. The winds blow down from the
Blue Mountains far to the northwest. The land gently rolls with the winds in all
directions. A few trees stand alone facing the weather. Various bushes create
the only true windbreaks along these highland plains.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
Small brown lumps of dirt pockmark the surface of the plains. Tiny dirt covered
trails dart in and out of the clumps of grass. Large bushes lie gathered
together against the winds. A few trees stand defiant, their branches dipping
and rising with the winds. The plains continue on in all directions.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
A large pool of mud occupies this small clearing in the bushes. A ancient oak
sprouts from its banks, and in return provides protection from the sun. Holly
bushes surrounds what must have been a pond at one time. The plains continue on
towards the northwest, trees occasionally defying the winds. The lands slowly
dip to the east, where a line of hill tops create a jagged horizon.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
The tall grass lies along the ground in large gathered clumps. The blades point
south towards the warmer climates. A few trees have managed to stand strong
against the winds that buffet these high plains. The land slowly rises to the
northwest and falls away to the southeast, where a line of hills break up the
horizon.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
A small patch of ground lies barren. The grasses grow thick around the edges,
with bushes gathering around. Nothing grows in the centre and the dirt lies
exposed. The rolling fields stretch far to the horizons, meeting a series of
hills far to the east.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
The grass grows short and the bushes seem stunted, however, the soil appears to
be very dark and rich. Grasses flow along the plains, quietly swept by the wind.
A stream meanders its way through the grasses, having started somewhere to the
east and quietly making its way to the hills in the southeast. Hills carved by
this and other similar streams decorate the horizon to the east.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
Limestone has melted away, creating a deep fissure in the plains above. The
sides of the crevice are irregular, containing many pockets. Bushes try in vain
to secure their roots to the crumbling rock. The canyon narrows to the west, but
begins to reveal a pattern of rocky outcroppings to the east. Steep limestone
walls make it difficult to discern the terrain to the north and south.
name: Deep Crevice

desc:
A huge boulder sits alone in a field of grass. No moss grows upon it, but there
are some dark stains on the eastern edge. At six feet tall, the boulder demands
noticing, but it seems out of place on these flat plains. A few trees stand
taller than it, but most of the bushes do not even come close to the same
height. It would be possible to roll it down hill to the east, but the ground
slopes upwards to the north and west.
name: Gentle Plains

desc:
Several large bushes decorate the plains with a lush green, a perfect hiding
place amongst the otherwise featureless plains. Tall grass leans against the
base of the bushes sitting on the outer edge of this patch. Three trees sit near
the middle providing a little shade. The plains are covered with fewer bushes in
all directions, however, a small line of trees can be seen far to the west.
name: Gentle Plains

desc:
The plains ascend to the north, covered with cranberry bushes. Nests of birds
can be seen sitting amongst the branches, cradled from the winds that sweep down
off the Blue Mountains located to the northwest. The plains continue to rise in
that direction. Grasses cover the land far to the east and south, but a deep
forest can be seen to the southwest.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
The magnificent scenery overwhelms you. Smooth hills descend below the tree-
line to the southeast. Small yellow flowers grow in a small carpet-like patch. A
trail zigzags its way to the north and west. The crest of the hill can be
followed into a deep brush forest to the east.
name: Breezy Plains

desc:
A small trickle of water flows from the spring quietly to the east, following
the contours of the high plains. The plains flow gently higher further to the
north and west. They drop slowly until the meet a ridge of hills to the east. A
few trees dot the horizon, their roots carpeted with the long grasses and varied
bushes that grow freely along the plains.
name: Isolated Plains

desc:
A solitary tree stands in the middle of the plains, surrounded by a series of
stumps jutting up through the tall grass. Many young saplings attempt to
establish a grove of oaks, bravely standing against the highland winds. A
stream, barely larger than a trickle weaves a path through the plains from east
to west. The plains continue far to the north and west, covered with trees in an
unorganised pattern. A dark line of hills can be seen cutting across the horizon
far to the east.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
A small furrow of limestone lies exposed. Thick grasses grow right up to the
edges, with some small grasses growing in the sandy soil between the ridges of
stone. A small tree attempts to create roots through the southern point, while a
large flowering bush occupies the other end, a scant six feet away. Gently
rolling plains continue on to the east and west, but a small grove of trees can
be seen far to the south.
name: Gentle Plains

desc:
This small dell is completely crowded by young oak saplings. Some short stubs of
older oak trees can be seen poking through the brush all over the dell. It seems
like someone may be caring for the cuttings, cutting down the old and replanting
with new. The underbrush is heavy, but very little dead wood or brush can be
found. Grassy plains seem to surround this small patch of oaks.
name: Small Dell

desc:
The remnant stumps of an oak forest cover the ground. The wood breaks away
freely, having been cut a long time ago. A few young saplings have taken root,
but not enough to replace the forest. The forest still survives to the west.
This land has become an extension of the vast grassy plains that extend far in
all other directions.
name: Oak Stumps

desc:
Foot long grass sways back and forth in the wind. Simblemynde flowers grow in
small patches scattered about. An occasional streak of limestone inhibit the
growth of plants. The edge of a deep forest stretches across the horizon to the
west, but the fairly flat grasslands continue in all other directions.
name: Dip in the Plains

desc:
A small pile of logs and debris litter the centre of the clearing. A fire once
burned here, but as you feel the cold ashes you realize, it must have been in
the distant past. Nothing else is remarkable about the small clearing. To the
north and east you can detect a hard dirt trail leading through the forest. To
the south though, a pile of boulders interspersed with the trees make that
direction not an option.
name: An Old Campsite

desc:
The thick bushes which dominate the trail lower on the hill have completely
given way here, on this steep slope. Higher up the trail, to the south of you,
the path becomes a little more level, though the land there seems to slope
upward from east to west, and in this rising land the track almost vanishes.
name: On a Steeply Sloping Trail

desc:
There is a gradual slope to these parts, leading into steeper mountains to your
south. By no means as high as the mighty peaks of Ered Luin, but still
formidable. Around you is flat land, some covered with grasses, other parts more
sparse and rocky. The Nan Duin, or valley river, flows east-west through the
region somewhere north of you. Small animals scurry around the place looking for
food.
name: Rocky Terrain

desc:
This large hill is not great in size, just high enough to stand above the
surrounding plains. There is grass covering the mound, and some deer play
happily off to one side. Just to your west, a shelf of rock has broken off,
making travel down that slope impossible. Below the small cliff stands a lone
farmhouse, looking neat and inhabited. You could get there by traveling
northwards a bit, skirting the rocks. On the other sides of you are flat-lands.
name: On a Large Hill

desc:
These are steeper slopes than other parts of the land around you, and take you
high above the small hills to your north. Southwards the way is blocked by a
sheet of granite rising vertically out of the ground. The chunks of rock that
were part of the cliff face are littering the ground around your feet. Below you
to the west, you spy a lone farmhouse, obviously inhabited as it seems to be
well kept. Below you to the east, are flat fields of grass and weeds.
name: Rising Mountains

desc:
This small ridge is apparently serving the purpose of connecting two small
mountain peaks. The term small is of course, in relation to the towering peaks
of the Ered Luin in the west. Looking down a small path, you see below you some
thin woods. By no means as thick as the forests in the northern parts of the
area, it does however hold a fair number of trees. This ridge ends to your east,
before plunging down a large cliff.
name: A Ridge through the Hills

desc:
This pit was maybe a mine used by dwarves looking for gold or gemstones.
Unfortunately, the walls of this cave do not appear any more than a reddish
hardened clay. A scattering of broken tools and even a badly dented lantern
litter the ground. The pit is cramped and the air still and stale.
name: A Pit under a Boulder

desc:
The path bends from north to west here. The trail looks fairly well used, and
could probably lead you to some civilization. This looks like the main path used
up here in Lhun for travellers. To the north-west you can see a huge gloomy
forest rise, while to the east you can see plains.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The pasture is rich in grass. Perfect for the livestock the farmer keeps. A
small farmhouse is to the south, neatly kept and sturdy. A little shed is
attached to the west wall of the dwelling, probably for storage of farming
supplies. Flowers grow next to the fence-posts, the only place a cow cannot
stick its big head. Through the fence, flat lands of rolling hills and wavy
grass can be seen.
name: Pasture

desc:
This is a simple farmhouse, not heavy on the decoration. Opting instead for
practical items to lay about, you sense a hardworking family lives here. A
butterchurn stands in the corner, and several pairs of old worn-out boots lie
just inside the door. Dishes are stacked on the table, and you can see food
goods lining the pantry. There is a small shed out the door to the west, while
to the north you can enter a pasture through another door.
name: A Small Farmhouse

desc:
Just a simple shed, used for storing farming supplies. Rakes and hoes are
hanging on the wall, along with shovels of various sizes. A metal bucket sits
unused in the corner. A narrow door leads out the east wall of the place,
probably into the farmhouse you saw earlier.
name: A Simple Shed

desc:
A small clearing in the trees provides a perfect place for this camp to have
been set up. A large circular site for campfires is located just off centre and
is surrounded by ample area for travellers to set up camp. A small pond offers
fresh water and a place to wash up and fill your skins.
name: Trail Camp

desc:
Massive trees appear in every direction, their trunks dominating entire
directions. For the most part you cannot see their tips, and are astounded at
how long they must have been here. Forest murmurs in all directions keep you
aware that you are not alone.
name: Great Forest

desc:
Massive trees appear in every direction, their trunks dominating entire
directions. For the most part you cannot see their tips, and are astounded at
how long they must have been here. Forest murmurs in all directions keep you
aware that you are not alone.
name: Great Forest

desc:
Massive trees appear in every direction, their trunks dominating entire
directions. For the most part you cannot see their tips, and are astounded at
how long they must have been here. Forest murmurs in all directions keep you
aware that you are not alone.
name: Great Forest

desc:
Massive trees appear in every direction, their trunks dominating entire
directions. For the most part you cannot see their tips, and are astounded at
how long they must have been here. Forest murmurs in all directions keep you
aware that you are not alone.
name: Great Forest

desc:
Massive trees appear in every direction, their trunks dominating entire
directions. For the most part you cannot see their tips, and are astounded at
how long they must have been here. Forest murmurs in all directions keep you
aware that you are not alone.
name: Great Forest

desc:
Massive trees appear in every direction, their trunks dominating entire
directions. For the most part you cannot see their tips, and are astounded at
how long they must have been here. Forest murmurs in all directions keep you
aware that you are not alone.
name: Great Forest

desc:
Hills still stretch out in all directions, but their numbers decline and each
hill seems to be the size of a small mountain. The land has much more definition
here and it is no longer easy to keep watch in all directions. A high ridge to
the distant north traces the horizon and several odd formations along its edge,
not quite natural, catches your eye. The path twists its way from the west to
the south.
name: Bend in the Worn Trail

desc:
The hills slowly flatten out and the grass gives way to small bushes and shrubs.
The familiar sounds of the river can still be heard to the south, but are
somewhat muffled by a growing number of thick trees and bushes that line the
riverbank.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
Large maples grow randomly about this entire area, their huge limbs sheltering
the sky from sight, and their bases still scarred from ancient milkings. A patch
of small evergreens grow off to your west, huddled close together trying to
survive amongst the much larger maples. Large multi-colored leaves are scattered
across the ground, and the faint smell of pine is in the air.
name: Grove of Maples

desc:
You are surrounded by tall bulky maple trees in all directions. The trees have
grown so closely together and are so huge that it makes it almost impossible to
see anything more than five meters away. To the south the number of trees
dwindles, opening up into a beautiful orchard. To the west the land emerges into
some flat-lands covered in grass and smaller bushes. You also see a small hill
rise up in that direction.
name: Dense Grove

desc:
Surrounded on four sides by massive stone walls, the feeling is one of
entombment. Examining the blocks reveals a much finer cut of stone than was used
to make the staircase. This was obviously an important figure indeed to have
such an elaborate tomb. The surprising thing about this place is, with so much
treasure here, there is no sarcophagus. Perhaps it was looted by earlier
grave-robbers.
name: The Bottom of a Dwarven Tomb

desc:
To your north is a massive cliff of loose rocks. It prevents any travel in that
direction and is very unstable. The land apparently chose this spot for a
geologic upheaval that left this odd-looking mound of rock. Up above it, you
think you see the beginnings of a flat range and hilly area. There is no access
from here though, as the cliff is so unstable.
name: Forest bordering the River

desc:
You are on a sandy strip of soil that runs along the bank of the Nan Duin. Just
to the north however, the clear space ends. After the few meters of beach, the
land suddenly sprouts massive trees. Looking past the first few trees, you see a
dark and gloomy forest. It looks spooky, but you hear the soft cry of birds in
there, making you realise that the place has at least a few harmless creatures.
name: Riverside Forest

desc:
The Nan Duin stretches out before you, winding slowly down to its junction with
the large river Luhn. Trees dot the area, as do grasses of various sizes. The
riverbanks are almost crowded with animals trying to get their fill of water,
and the birds wait anxiously in the tree for some bathing water. To your north,
a wall of stone arises, but the way is clear to the east and west.
name: Forest bordering the River

desc:
You are now on a small trail that seems to have very infrequent use. It is
covered by gravel and pebbles of different sizes, interspersed with a brown hard
soil. Just south, you see a sturdy bridge crossing over the Nan Duin. The path
seems to continue to the north, towards distant lands. In the west you can see a
massive forest that has a gloomy appearance. Perhaps getting closer to it would
make it seem less threatening.
name: An Earthen Path

desc:
From your vantage point on top of the bridge, you can see in every direction.
Flowing east-west under the bridge, is the swift Nan Duin. To your north you can
detect a small earth path leading towards distant lands. Only grassy plains are
visible to the south, their wavy grasses blowing free in the wind.
name: On a Bridge

desc:
You stand upon a grassy hill just south of a large bridge. A cool breeze blows
through your hair. You observe insects hard at work buzzing around various wild
flowers, and birds taking some of the long dry grass in their beaks. Looking to
the south you see expanses of fields and farms spreading majestically. Rolling
hills lead into thick forest-land to your west. While to the north is the river
with a small bridge over it.
name: Grassy Hill

desc:
The lands around you are simple yet diverse. The barrage of colour comes not
from green trees, or bright flowers, yet is still present in the form of golden
stalks of grass, brown rocky soil, and pale gray rocks. Less friendly lands lie
to your west, where a large forest begins. You cannot enter it here however, as
the trees interspersed with vines makes passage impossible. In the north, you
can hear the faint sound of running water. Looking to the south you see the
trail used by the dwarven merchants, selling in the elven ports the iron goods
produced in the Blue Mountains.
name: Grassy Lands

desc:
The refreshing Nan Duin is to your north, flowing briskly along its east-west
course through the mountains. The flat-land here is grassy and dry, except for
where the water meets the land. Rocks dot the area in small clumps, and there
are small trees grouped together. Southwards you spy more grassy plains, and to
the west a large forest looms in the distance.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
A line of apple trees borders the Nan Duin and offers quite a bit of shade. A
squirrel has decided to take residence in one of the large trunks, apparently
happy with the days search for food. The water flows just to your north, while
southwards the terrain gets more rocky. Open plains are on both your east and
west sides.
name: An Orchard by the River

desc:
The land is less fertile here, due mostly to the many rocks and pebbles in the
soil. Growing things here is not impossible however, as evidenced by the annual
grass growing long and wavy. More flat land is on all sides of you, rising
slightly to your west in the form of a small hill. The hill is round and would
offer only a moderately better view. In the north you hear water.
name: Rocky Terrain

desc:
You are walking through wide plains south of the Nan Duin. The plains continue
to the north and the terrain looks a bit rougher to the west. These rolling
plains grow good grass, the kind that is long and brown.
name: Open Plains

desc:
Vast stretches of plains surround you. The splashing of the Nan Duin comes from
the north, where it flows west-east towards the Luhn. The plains continue to the
south and the tops of hills can be spotted to the southwest. Movement here
proves a little difficult as many rocks and piles of rubble clutter the ground.
The rising Lhun Mountains can be seen to the south.
name: Open Plains

desc:
The land here is bleak and empty save for the dark forest to the west. The
plains in this area are different than the plains of southern Eriador, as their
fertility is decreased due to the extreme rockiness of the soil. That has not
hindered the wavy grass however, as it grows thick and long. More of the same
grassy fields can be seen to the south and east. The Nan Duin flows swiftly to
your north.
name: Sparse Plains

desc:
You see a small opening in the vegetation to the west and south. The darkness
feels like a blanket suffocating you, as there is little light that can enter
the forest because of the dense tree-tops. To the north, travel is inhibited by
massive clumps of dead brush and trees. Even as so much dead vegetation covers
the ground, the forest is full of life and fragrances. Animals scurry about in
their daily quest for food.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
The forest to the west becomes increasingly tangled as you try to push your way
through. The branches seem to have a mind of their own as they try to inhibit
your progress. The ever-present darkness decreases your vision considerably. To
the south there appears a break in the dense forest. It is only a small
clearing, but perhaps it will lead to more open areas. The sounds of animals and
birds fill the air. The way east is blocked by impassable mounds of dead
vegetation and fallen trees.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
Thick branches and sharp thorns brush up against your legs and arms. You are
constantly probed by the long vines and gnarled limbs of the trees. In every
direction there is more of the same gloomy forest. It grows dense and choking
all around you. Only towards the west does there seem to be any break in the
growth. A small clearing is nestled there, amongst the foliage.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
The growth of trees, bushes, and vines is increasingly abundant here. So thick
even, that you have to scramble under and over bushes to make any headway.
Travel to the south is not just difficult, it is impossible. bushes mingled with
the dense foliage makes traffic impossible in this direction. Cries of animals
pierce the heavy silence in the forest, some crying for food, others echoing the
last scream of death.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
Loud growls circle around you and you feel as if you are definitely not alone.
The forest seems to surround you here, as it grows thick on your east, west, and
south. Northwards the way is blocked by some abnormally large piles of deadfall.
The west seems a bit more clear, and you hear the sound of running water from
that direction. It is probably the mighty Nan Duin.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
On three sides the forest surrounds you, with only north offering any other
choice. In that direction you see the Nan Duin meandering throughout the Blue
Mountains. Just south is a less wooded part of the forest, sort of a clearing.
On the east and west sides of you however, there is a dark and threatening
woodland, that seems to exude danger.
name: Forest by the River

desc:
The darkness in the forest overwhelms you, but you see glimpses of glowing eyes
peering at you intently. For brief moments you can hear growls as packs of
wolves begin to take hold of your scent. Will they leap out at you or slowly
follow you until you bed down for the night? Any opening in the forest to the
Nan Duin is covered by a very dense vegetation that is hard to push through, but
manageable.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
The forest grows thick and wild here. So much so that it slowly loses its
forest-like appearance. Vines have covered some of the trees, making it seem as
though the trunks around you are one entity. Light can barely be seen through
the dense tree tops, and even if you are blessed with keen powers of sight in
the darkness, visibility is of a short length. To the east however, the growth
seems to thin slightly.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
You have stepped into a small clearing in the forest. Around you on all sides
are dense trees interspersed with vines and rockpiles. The thickest parts of the
forest lie to the east, west, and south, with only the northern direction
offering any variety in surroundings. To that direction, you hear the sound of
running water, probably the mighty Nan Duin. Looking up in the trees, you see
some rather large nests. Though a source of protection for the birds that
inhabit them, they would not support your size and weight.
name: Clearing in the Forest

desc:
The forest here grows dense and dark. The high treetops are as thick as the
branches towards the ground, making the penetration of sunlight or moonlight
very difficult. The dim light afforded you allows detection of a small trail to
the south. On all other sides however, only more gloomy forest awaits. The howl
of wolves and the screech of birds punctuates the heavy silence.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
Many trees cover this area, interfering with your sense of direction. You chop
off branches and vines in hope of finding a way through this dense mess. Ground
foliage is made primarily of mushrooms, fern-type plants, and hardy flowers.
This is because all of them have a relatively low light necessity, and can grow
in the shade that the treetops produce. A particularly large pile of dead
branches catch your eye. Peering intently at the pile, you swear there is noise
coming from it.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
The area you are in gives plenty of variety now. All around you are samples of
the various terrain to be found in this large region. To the south is the Nan
Duin, which transects the mountains in an east-west direction. On your north and
west sides, woodlands grow in varying densities, ranging from chokingly thick,
to sparse and roomy. Tall grass grows on the ground when not smothered out by
larger foliage. The eastward direction holds a small trail, leading to distant
lands.
name: Forest bordering the River

desc:
The trees grow increasingly large as they grow closer to the Nan Duin, making
travel more difficult. The river flows just to the south, but your chances of
reaching it from here are small. Huge boulders interspersed with dense clumps of
shrub prevent you from proceeding easily. Small birds peer down at you in
wonder, and you hear the sound of a woodpecker busy at work.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
The forest borders the mighty Nan Duin here. It is flowing merrily along just to
your south. You cannot go northwards here, as a pile of boulders has mixed with
the trees and vines, making travel impossible. The forest extends to your east
and west, leading into more gloom and darkness. The sounds of forest animals
reach your ears, mostly small animals, but occasionally a large thud or snap can
be heard, signifying a larger beast.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
The Nan Duin stretches before you and you take a deep breath and inhale the
fresh air. Many frogs and toads leap around your feet as you observe a family of
deer drink from the river. The scene is peaceful, much more peaceful than the
sight of the dense forest to your north. Perhaps harmless, but heavily overgrown
areas like that have often been the habitat of less-than-friendly beings. The
river is to the south, flowing east-west over the Blue Mountains.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
This part of the forest seems darker than the rest, probably due to the
entanglement of the trees above you. Even in the dim light, you can detect a
less thick region to your north, and a set of claw-like footprints that lead off
to your east. Looking closer, you see an odd structure of some sort. Not exactly
a building, but humanoid construction for certain. An owl's glowing eyes divert
your attention for a moment as you continue to hear sounds of wildlife around
you.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
If you are so inclined, this grassy space just off the trail seems like a nice
place to take a rest. Ferns, long grass, and short trees scatter the area close
at hand, while much taller and thicker woodlands lie to the south. Just east of
here, that earthen trail continues, leading into lands many miles away.
name: On the Side of the Trail

desc:
To the west the hill descends into the Narbrethil Forest. Recounting tales of
the place, you are thoughtful of the stories of ghastly beasts and lost hunting
parties. Rather than believe these stories, you look with an eye of wisdom at
the place, and decide that instead of mighty creatures, the people got lost
within the massive trees and gripping vines. The east reveals long stretches of
grass leading to a small trail.
name: A Small Hill

desc:
To the west is the ominous Narbrethil Forest with trees peeking high amongst the
clouds. The east brings in more grasslands and to the south a cliff begins to
ascend to the top of a hill. In the north, rolling plains with some large
canyons are in view. A particularly deep canyon prevents immediate travel in
that direction.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
You notice the forest appears to be a bit darker here, but you manage to uncover
a small track that leads to the south. There are a few footprints on the ground,
obviously old, but large and a bit intimidating nonetheless. Small caterpillars
and other insects roam about casually, and sounds of small animals can be heard
as they rustle about in the thick growth.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
A lovely trail of flowers borders a group of bushes. To the north, a great
canyon has opened up, leaving no option for travel to that direction. Southwards
holds a bit more variety, as it forms a cliff of loose rock, about five meters
high. There is a danger in climbing it, for the stones are unstable and could
come down upon your head.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The grass blows in the wind and you come across an occasional group of bushes
that seem out of place in these vast grasslands. What catches your attention
most however, are the large roots rising out of the ground. They lead to the
south, past trees that try to block your entry into the place. If one tries hard
however, the tangles and roots can be overcome if one really wants to proceed
into the dark woods.
name: Uprooted Grasslands

desc:
A few rabbits hurry into the forest at the sight of you. The land here is rough,
buckled and cracked in places as if something lives beneath you. As you examine
the ground you come across large roots, enormous actually, that lead to the
forest to the south and west. A couple of deer peer angrily at you as they nudge
their fawn to the nearby pond. You may enter the forest to your south, or
continue into the grass to your east.
name: Rough Woods

desc:
The lush greenery is a refreshing sight. Rabbits, squirrels, and other small
rodents scurry between the bushes, making use of the foliage to build shelters
and attain food. Birds sing songs that fill the air with a gay melody. As they
tend to their young in the trees that stretch far above, you hear high pitched
cries signifying the newborn birds perpetual hungriness. You hear the familiar
murmur of the Nan Duin to the south.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
The temperature in here is higher than that above ground. Perfect you decide,
for a nesting place for animals. The bones on the floor are indicative that
something else has had a similar idea. Scraps of flesh litter the floor, and the
smell is dank and foetid. The only way out is back the way you came.
name: Wolf Den

desc:
The dark and gloomy hole is filled with whimpering sounds that seem to come from
the south. The smell of rotten flesh invades your nostrils and almost makes you
choke. The tree above seems to encroach here, as you see thick roots protruding
below the surface into this narrow crawlspace. Another dark room is barely
visible to your south.
name: Muddy Hole

desc:
A beautiful garden is here, comprised of wildflowers of captivating colors. The
fragrance is lively, somewhere between fruity and spicy. Insects are drawn to
the bright colours, and buzz happily about the many different kinds of growth. A
thick green hedge, presumably to keep the local herbivores out, surrounds the
garden and prohibits travel anywhere but back the way you came.
name: A Backyard Garden

desc:
This is little more than a sprawling hut situated on the bank of a peaceful
slow-moving river. Part stone, part sod, the hut leans drunkenly against a tall,
thick tree. Its roof is equally haphazard, composed of large branches, wooden
plates, and gaping holes. The view out the west window is vast, as it reveals
the Nan Duin making its slow trek downstream. There are doors to both the west
and north. Otherwise the decor is spartan.
name: A Hunting Cabin

desc:
At a small intersection now, you are offered two choices. The earthen trail
still runs north and south here, but a small patch of grass is just to one side
of it. To your east is an overgrown path, leading to a circle of stones atop a
large hill in the north. A huge iron gate, wedged between two boulders, makes
the way impassable unless you have the key to the gate.
name: An Earthen Path

desc:
The trail continues to the east and south here. On occasion you notice a few
small animals run from one side of the path to the other to the safety of the
forest. The forest may hold safety for them, but as you look into the dark
shadows of the trees, you wonder if it will be dangerous to go in there. A
bird's cry echoes from within the bounds of the woodlands.
name: An Earthen Path

desc:
The path bends from the west toward the north. On your east lies a large hill,
with a circle of stones arranged in a ring on its top. The stones are obviously
hand placed. You cannot scramble up the slope here, for this side of it has
broken away, leaving only a crumbling cliff standing. To attempt a climb up this
would be futile, as the rocks would surely tumble down upon you.
name: An Earthen Path

desc:
The trail stops here, mandated by the fact that a gorge has opened up to your
north. Not having a bridge here, it is totally impossible to cross. On your east
and west sides however, the plains of this region continue. A well-trodden earth
path leads away southwards, heading back towards the river. Bunnies hop
playfully close to the edge of the chasm, daring the loose rocks.
name: An Earthen Path

desc:
The path seems to dead-end here, as it meets a dense hedgerow, which sweeps
around the small hill, to the south, apparently enclosing it completely. Atop
this hill, a circle of stones can be seen, which seem to have been laid out
deliberately, to a careful design. Even at this distance, carvings can be seen
on some of them.
name: Bleak Plains

desc:
You are standing on a small hill, made mostly of solid rocks and some sandy
soil. Examining the stones around you, you see that they are placed about three
metres apart, and are arranged in a ring. Each stone is about a metre high, and
a metre wide. In the centre of the ring, a massive slab covers the ground. It is
completely smooth, and has a short inscription on it. Around the base of the
hill, a dense encircling hedgerow has been planted.
name: Amon Othar

desc:
You are standing in some long wavy grass, just on the bend of an old path. It
goes in a north-south direction, and lies to the east and south of where you are
now. More rolling hills and grass seem to be the topography of this area, but
forested areas lie south of the trail. The grass blows in the wind like a
million dead arms.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The inky blackness embraces you as the stairs descend into the depths of Amon
Othar. Rough hewn blocks of stone construct the walls, leading you to believe
that it is Dwarven work. You cannot see the landing of the steps yet, leaving
you only two options. Either back up the stairs into the outdoors, or down into
the unknown.
name: A Steep Staircase

desc:
The bare white walls of the small antechamber stand straight and tall, barring
any passage except due east, where a small dark corridor can be seen, leading
off into the darkness. The walls are void of decoration, but small structural
features are present, that would create shallow shadows dancing upon the wall.
The stone floor of the chamber is carefully laid, using different shades of
coloured stones, thus creating streaks of colour and images.
name: Small Antechamber

desc:
Now behind one of the grey walls, you find yourself in a narrow room with a low
ceiling. Used most likely as a second treasure room, the blocks of stone here
are of the same fine cut. A strange thing about this room, as soon as you
entered it, a feeling of great tension came over you. It was as if your presence
awakened a long-dormant power.
name: A Secret Passage

desc:
