The ancient corridor gently slopes down to the east, and climbs to the west,
where it ends in a barely visible chamber. The white stone walls are smooth to
the touch, with little sign of moisture. The only features that give a clear
hint of progress while moving along the passage are the tiled floor and some
small holes, regularly spaced at the top of the walls and along the ceiling.
name: Long Corridor

desc:
The corridor is an arched passage through the mountain, leading east, further
into the ancient tomb, and west towards the entrance. The walls are burned and
blackened by furious flames in several places and most of the white stonework
has been cracked and has fallen to the ground in small splinters as a mockery of
its former glory. High upon the walls there are small holes, placed with an even
spacing, their rims black and ragged by the burning inferno that once raged
unchecked here. Lining the ceiling, similar holes can be seen all along the
passage. The floor of the corridor was once a tiled floor, the stones laid with
great precision to form a beautiful hallway, but now they are encrusted with
remnants of molten white stonework and blackened by blazing fires. Upon the
floor lie charred and rusted remnants of old weapons, some of which are partly
melted.
name: Blackened Corridor

desc:
Deep below the mountains lies this small and carved hallway. Carefully created
by master craftsmen the chamber has a floor laid with stone tiles, and white
walls that are polished smooth by caring hands. The white masonry of the walls
is only broken by a heavy lever, placed in a dark crack on the eastern wall.
name: Carved Hall

desc:
The small cubicle is shaped as a semicircle, the wall almost perfectly round.
The white wall is smooth and polished, made by master craftsmen; the ceiling is
flat, made of somewhat darker stone. Being a very small chamber, the cubicle
more resembles a niche, created to place a statue in. Placed on the southern
wall there is a small lever, fastened in a small, dark crack. Marring the white
surface of the room are small streaks of red and upon the floor there are dark
red spots covering the stone.
name: Cramped Cubicle

desc:
A large stone grave is standing on the floor in the middle of the room. The
place is well kept and the tapestries on the wall remind you of the room you
just came from. These tapestries and pictures however, seem to be centreed on a
particular individual. Although many dwarves are seen in the pictures, one dwarf
is seen in them all. You conclude that he must be the resident of the tomb. As
his picture depicts a large, battle scarred Dwarf, you wonder if disturbing his
final resting spot is a wise decision. Upon the tomb are some words etched in
the stone.
name: A Hidden Tomb

desc:
Several boulders lie about the field, looking like they may have once been
arranged in some meaningful pattern. Large bushes obscure the view of the
surrounding fields. The land gently rises to the northwest until it meets a line
of dark blue hills. The cool winds whistle through the boughs of the trees
scattered along the plains.
name: Rising Plains

desc:
This broad plateau is covered in prickly briar plants. Walking through the
closely knit bushes is difficult, as well as somewhat painful. The numerous
rabbits inhabiting the briar patch dart about with ease, well protected from
hawks and other predators by the thorny branches. With some effort, you can make
your way east to a trail through the hills, or travel south to the the crest of
a hill.
name: Thorny Briars

desc:
The northern winds have swept the plains clean. Weeds grow up between the cracks
formed between the boulders. One particularly large rock provides a good
viewpoint of the surrounding terrain. The hill along the ridge continues to the
east and west, but makes a gradual descent to the south. The plains continue to
the north through a field of dark-green briars.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
The constant winds blowing across these plains make the grasses and thick brush
sway in the breeze. The bushes provide some protection from the winds. The land
gently rolls on to the south and east. A small rise to the west overlooks a
grove of ancient oaks. The foothills of the Blue Mountains rise above the
horizon far to the northwest.
name: Windswept Plains

desc:
On the underside of the bridge a thick moss grows, obviously in response to the
constant moisture supplied by the river. It is relatively dark under the bridge,
as the space between the boards of the bridge and the river is quite small. You
see many loose boards on the bridge, while others appear quite strong. The Nan
Duin flows contentedly in its usual east-west direction.
name: Under a Bridge

desc:
The river banks here consist of solid stone; whether natural or otherwise they
provide the perfect location for the construction of the wooden bridge you see
overhead. The builders obviously knew that this stone would resist the river's
ability to erode, and that this bridge would stand upright for many years to
come. The river narrows here and the stone banks provide sturdy footing to rest
and water ones horses. The river flows from west to east.
name: Beneath the Wooden Bridge

desc:
The tunnel ends abruptly, only a few paces from the large cavern lying north of
here, in a large pool of water. This is the armoury's water supply but it seems
to have been left unused for a long period of time, as roots have grown in the
wet tunnel, partly blocking access to the water. The water is dark but clear,
showing few signs of the bottom below.
name: Watery Tunnel

desc:
Large oak and pine furniture is placed in an orderly fashion all around the
interior of the alehouse. A fire burns in the huge fireplace along the east
wall. A bear fur covers the floor in front of the fire. Oak barrels cover the
northern wall, containing the varied beverages sold here. A large oak bar
covered with wood carvings keeps the customers away from the barrels.
name: Roadside Alehouse

desc:
A bright light emanates from the fireplace, generating enough heat to fill the
room. A large table covers the centre of the room. Bowls, pitchers and cutlery
lie scattered about, apparently in use by the kitchen staff. Several barrels sit
in a rack along the eastern wall. The equipment seems sturdy, but lacks the
detail to be of much value. A pair of double doors leads into the common room.
name: Kitchen

desc:
Carved limestone walls surround deep valleys. Much of the cliff sides have
crumbled leaving their remains along the steep hillside. Small shrubs grow
amongst the boulders and stones. The rocky lands continue to the north and
south. A trail can be seen to the east, where the canyons open to a vast plain.
name: Rocky Hillside

desc:
Varied branches cover the vast floor of the hollow cave. The branches appear to
have been torn from large shrubs. The leaves have been pressed down by a heavy
weight. Wild berries add colour to the greys and browns of the surrounding
walls. The cavern seems to have been enlarged by large claws. Light leaks
through the thick vegetation covering the doorway.
name: Bear's Den

desc:
The tunnel walls are covered with tiny streams of water, trickling down to the
floor. There they pool together and flow to the south. A patch of earth and
roots hang from above. Small webs decorate the edges. The tunnel seems to have
been carved into the limestone long ago. A cool breeze comes from the south.
name: Ancient Tunnel

desc:
Ancient lime stones have been carved and worn smooth by the passing of water
that must have once flowed here. A few chunks have broken away from the walls
since. Various cracks decorate the stone above. Some evidence of seepage can be
seen. A soft glow from a larger cavern can be seen to the west.
name: Hidden Passage

desc:
Many glittering rocks, crystals and other loose minerals are scattered on the
ground. Several of the glowing crystals appear to have been collected and placed
in piles to give some concentrated light in special places. A completely rusted
lantern lies on the ground giving evidence that sometime in the past someone has
visited this place. Rushing water can be heard to the south.
name: Glittering Caves

desc:
This quiet inlet is almost completely surrounded by lush green forest. The water
from the river slowly creeps in from the west, and there is a small rocky beach
on its north side. The branches from the huge trees extend well out into the
middle of the inlet, scattering multi-coloured leaves that float in slow
rhythmic swirls.
name: Tranquil Inlet

desc:
Huge boulders are strewn all about, making the trail quite tiresome to follow.
Rocky hills rear high to all sides, but the path picks a way between them,
turning here from west to south. An eerie silence seems to fill the air in an
all-enveloping gloom.
name: Along the Rocky Trail

desc:
You barely make your way around the boulders here, trying to keep track of the
so-called path, which seems to stumble haltingly through the hills. In all
directions rugged hills climb away from you, their summits covered with rocks,
their slopes with loose scree.
name: Rocky Trail

desc:
You are walking among some grassy hilltops. There is no special path to walk
upon here, but you travel fast and easy here anyway. The air seems to carry a
strange feeling to you. Your further exploration to the north is blocked by a
huge gorge making it impossible to travel there, while to the south you can see
more small hills.
name: Low Hills

desc:
You are standing between small hills covered with green grass. The hills climb
and fall endlessy. To the south of you the hills look more rocky. This
mountainous terrain makes you pretty exhausted, as you put much effort in moving
around, up and down all these small hilltops. The hills look higher to the
south. To the far south you can see a forest.
name: Low Hills

desc:
The hills here have become much rougher and rockier. You have to move very
slowly up here, twisting your way between large boulders and stones. The
surrounding area looks quite wasted and dead, but to the north you notice more
hills and some of them are even covered with green grass.
name: Rocky Hills

desc:
The hills here have become much rougher and rocky. The so-called path you tried
to follow is impossible to keep track of due to all the rocks and boulders you
constantly have to make your way around. To the south you can see a forest, and
you can also see a river flowing lazily southwards. To the north however, you
can only see more hills.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
The interior of the cabin is large, as would be expected from Dwarven
construction. The furnishings are spartan, indicative of a worker who is not
home much. A table sits in the corner, along with two chairs and a footstool.
The only other item of interest is a large sharpening wheel in the centre of the
room. It is a stone wheel, powered by a crank on one side. The double edged axe
that hangs from the wall suggests that this a lumber-jack hut.
name: A Dwarven Home

desc:
The trees stretch high above you, and the undergrowth is only slight. The sound
of rushing water is louder here and you guess there must be a river nearby. The
vegetation is so thick to the south that you have no chance of finding a way
there. The only exit is back east.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
You have come to a little clearing in the forest. There are no trees around
here, except for one big old tree, standing on the edge of the river flowing
right to the south of it. The tree seems to be big enough to carry your weight.
To the south a big river is slowly making its way southwards.
name: At a Large Tree

desc:
The land swells here forming a deep bowl-shaped valley completely covered in
lush green grass and the occasional sapling, with the western perimeter lined
with a multitude of rich colored flowers. The air is damp, full of watery mist,
and if it were not for the thunderous roar of rushing water, this place would be
very peaceful.
name: Damp Forest

desc:
The forest floor beneath these green giants is thick with shadows, the faint
streamers of light let through by gaps between the thick burly branches rapidly
disappear at the first signs of twilight. Eyes dart out from every dark corner,
mischievous and curious, watching your every move with the utmost interest.
name: Forest of Shadows

desc:
The forest floor beneath these green giants is thick with shadows, the faint
streamers of light let through by gaps between the thick burly branches rapidly
disappear at the first signs of twilight. Eyes dart out from every dark corner,
mischievous and curious, watching your every move with the utmost interest.
name: Forest of Shadows

desc:
The forest floor beneath these green giants is thick with shadows, the faint
streamers of light let through by gaps between the thick burly branches rapidly
disappear at the first signs of twilight. Eyes dart out from every dark corner,
mischievous and curious, watching your every move with the utmost interest.
name: Forest of Shadows

desc:
The trail seems to vanish abruptly, leaving no signs as to where it could
possibly continue. A careful scan of the immediate surroundings eventually
reveals the remains of an old path, almost hidden in the grasses. This trail
leads into the shadows to the north. To the west, the terrain becomes more open,
and you can glimpse the approach to a clifftop. To the east, the forest
stretches out before you.
name: Trail through the Forest

desc:
You stand on the edge of the cliff overlooking the slow flowing river beneath
you to the west. It is a long way down and a slight case of vertigo temporarily
causes you to freeze and cautiously take a few steps back, hugging the closest
tree. The cliff face across the river extends a few yards above you making it
difficult to survey, but you do notice a single large stump that grows near the
cliff's edge. You peek over the edge one last time and wonder if all this
adventure and exploration is really worth it.
name: Cliff's Edge

desc:
It seems you have stumbled onto an enclosed alcove that has been adopted by a
family of grizzly bears. The room's ventilation is very poor as a must hangs in
the air, making it quite difficult to breathe. Debris and food scraps litter the
floor and claw marks cover the entire back wall. The bears seem to be very
hungry, and very happy to see you.
name: Dark Alcove

desc:
You have made your way up in the tree. There are many huge branches here, making
you feel secure. Maybe this would be a good resting place. To the north you get
a view of the forest. It seems to clear up not far to the north, and you see
some mountainous terrain through the haze.
name: Up in a Tree

desc:
The cave has been cleanly carved into the stone of the mountain. A wood bed
occupies the eastern wall, great furs heaped on it. A fire burns in a carved
fireplace, the smoke slowly curling up through a crevice above. The floor is
covered with a giant fur, from some animal that does not live close by. Great
carvings cover an ancient hardwood desk.
name: Inside the Dwarven Home

desc:
This armoury is set up in a low-roofed cavern and is even more secretive and
hidden than the one you previously visited. A few fireplaces, benches and tool
racks are spaced out across the floor. The tools and ovens seem larger and more
detailed, allowing for more complex and delicate manufacturing.
name: Inner Armoury

desc:
The tunnel is completely filled with dark, cold water that flows swiftly to the
east. The constant rush of water keeps the rocks completely bare on its path
through the mountain.
name: Underground River

desc:
Here the small footpath continues through the trees, now more visible and clear
with only a few leaves and twigs fallen upon its path. It is strange however,
that you can faintly make out numerous footprints leading north, but none return
from that direction.
name: Forest Byway

desc:
Small stones lie spread across the ground beneath the thin grass. A few bushes
and even fewer trees have managed to grow up through the stony soil. Larger
rocks can be occasionally seen. The grassy plains continue far to the south and
west. A steep loosely covered hillside leads down to the east. It would be too
dangerous to traverse.
name: Stony Plateau

desc:
Huge boulders line the stony paths leading through these hills. Brush grows
thickly along the washed-out trails. Grasses grow softly beneath the shadows of
the large rocks, perhaps safe from the stones collapse. The rim of a plateau can
be seen circling around the hills to the west and south. Water has worn paths
deeper into the hills to the east.
name: Stony Foothills

desc:
The waters of a side channel of the River Lhun flow lazily southward here. To
the east is a small island, actually part of a long bar of sand and mud that
separates this backwater from the miles-wide expanse of the main channel. At
times, however, when the river is in flood, its waters must overflow the bar and
create a single vast raging torrent; this explains the steep, undercut bank
which rises impassibly to the west of you, where earth and trees have been
ripped away. To the north, where the water flows over the mud bar, it is too
shallow to swim and the bed is too soft and treacherous to walk.
name: River Lhun

desc:
You are in the western channel of the River Lhun, which is separated from the
river's eastern part by a miles-long bar of sand and mud. North and south of
your location the bar is overgrown with foliage, but to the east of you the
river's waters wash over the bar.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river passes just west of a large island. It flows lazily to the south from
the north. You can hear a bird's song linger in the air.
name: River Lhun

desc:
The river narrows to the north. To the east you can see a tributary meld with
the river. The water is very clear here, and you can easily see the sandy bottom
beneath you. Small fish dart in and out among rocks, and green aquatic plants
sway in the current.
name: A Small Tributary of the River

desc:
Many picks, shovels, and other abandoned mining tools litter the ground. This
cave was obviously a mine used by the dwarves in a quest for iron or other
precious metals. Unfortunately, the walls of this cave do not appear any more
than a reddish hardened clay. The smell coming from the east smells like rotten
flesh and would only appeal to an evil soul.
name: Abandoned Mine Shaft

desc:
The smell of half eaten flesh fills your lungs and makes you choke. The floor is
littered with decaying corpses that seem to be deer, elk and the like. You
shiver at the thought that whatever lives here may decide to make you its next
meal. You do not want to end up as a pile of unrecognizable remains, perhaps you
had better leave.
name: Bear Hideaway

desc:
You have to climb the rockface for the last few meters, scuffing the soft rock
walls with the soles of your boots as you acend. Atop the cliff you stand on a
pale white stone slab from which the entire forestland can be seen. The huge
stump is even bigger than it seemed from across the river, its immense size
dominating this entire ridge. After closer inspection you notice only minor
indications of age and decay and marvel at nature's ability to preserve her own
as you know this stump must be generations and generations old.
name: Ridge Overlooking the River

desc:
A thick cluster of trees grows along the edge of the cliff, kept moist by the
constant flow of the river below, their thick canopy casting the ground into
deep shadow. Dead foliage and decomposing leaves cover the ground, releasing a
pervasive odour of decay. Above, high atop the largest tree in the vicinity, a
small sheltered shack has been skilfully constructed.
name: Foresty Darkness

desc:
Although very small and somewhat ragged, you feel very comfortable sitting and
staring out the windows of this little shack. You are yards above all the rest
of the trees in the forest, and have a clear view of the land in all directions.
name: Tree-top Outpost

desc:
Water flows from the west, turning south in this underground river. The mass of
water fills the tunnel completely and the constant flowing makes swimming
difficult. The rock walls are mostly black, but there are a few lighter patches
here and there. The stream seems to resurface slightly eastwards.
name: Underground River

desc:
Rising a few inches above the water, a rock allows weary explorers some ground
to rest on. Water washes the rock constantly, making it slippery and wet. The
massive walls are dark and damp, with patches of fungal growth. Darkness lies
above, concealing the roof somewhere up in the shaft.
name: Underground Island

desc:
A small tunnel coming from the east ends in a pool of water here. This tunnel
seems to have been unused for a long period of time, as roots have grown in the
wet tunnel and are blocking much of the water. The water is dark but clear,
showing few signs of the bottom below.
name: Water Supply
